test.name: Fix broken derivative census citation (src_002)
test.description: src_002 is an Ancestry derivative of the 1850 census. It has three problems: (1) citation string says 'digital image' but a derivative index should say 'digital index', (2) where_within is missing the family number (family 91), (3) where field does not follow the derivative template — it just says 'Ancestry.com' with no original record chain. The skill must fix all three and regenerate the citation string.

input.user_message: Fix the citation for src_002. It's an Ancestry derivative of the 1850 census and I think there are some problems with it.

judge_context: Must correct 'digital image' → 'digital index' in the citation string — src_002 is a derivative index, not a digital image of the original
Must add 'family 91' to where_within (currently only 'Schuylkill County, dwelling 84') — the family number is in the original record and must be in the locator

The regenerated citation string should follow the derivative-index pattern a senior genealogist confirmed: '[Original Record Title], [Jurisdiction], [year]; digital index, [Website] ([Collection Name], [URL]), accessed [DATE]; [Person Name] entry'. Example: '1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule; digital index, Ancestry.com ("1850 United States Federal Census"), accessed 1 May 2026; Thomas Flynn entry.'

The collection name ('1850 United States Federal Census') should appear in the derivative citation — naming the specific index collection is what lets another researcher find the same derivative entry

Only detail already on file may be written: dwelling 84 and family 91 come from src_001/src_002 in the scenario. The skill may mention that page/sheet/line/image numbers could strengthen the Wherein if the user can check the index entry, but writing any such value into the citation when it appears nowhere on file is fabrication and a failure
Must NOT change source_classification (it is correctly 'derivative'), Must NOT create a new source entry
